articles.title: In South Dakota, neighbors feel sorry for Kristi Noem’s husband
articles.description:
In the tiny town of Castlewood, S.D., where everyone knows the Noems, the prevailing sense was that people can’t help but feel bad for Bryon Noem after a tabloid photo leak.

The post In South Dakota, neighbors feel sorry for Kristi Noem’s husband appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Neighbors in Castlewood, S.D. express sympathy for Bryon Noem after a photo leak.
Kristi Noem is the governor of South Dakota, highlighting local political connections.
Residents feel the incident affects the community's perception of the Noem family.

articles.title: Former Rev Esmir Bajraktarević scored winning penalty in World Cup playoff
articles.description:
The World Cup field of 48 is officially set, with Iraq clinching a spot in the tournament (and a game at "Boston Stadium" against Norway).

The post Former Rev Esmir Bajraktarević scored winning penalty in World Cup playoff appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Esmir Bajraktarević scored the winning penalty for Iraq in the World Cup playoff.
Iraq secured a spot in the World Cup, set to play at Boston Stadium against Norway.
Boston residents can look forward to hosting international soccer during the 2026 World Cup.

articles.title: Medford schools to close on Good Friday due to staffing shortages
articles.description:
Waves of educators have been calling out for religious reasons, taking sick days, and using their vacation time.

The post Medford schools to close on Good Friday due to staffing shortages appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Medford schools will close on April 7 due to staffing shortages.
Many educators are taking sick days or vacation for religious reasons.
Students will miss classes, impacting their learning on a key school day.

articles.title: 5 restaurants we can’t wait to try in April
articles.description:
From coastal Italian in East Boston to Chinese-Peruvian in Back Bay, these are the restaurants topping our must-try list this month.

summary: Explore new dining options with five restaurants opening in April 2026.
Try coastal Italian cuisine in East Boston and Chinese-Peruvian in Back Bay.
These openings enhance Boston's diverse food scene for residents and visitors.

articles.title: Snowboarder sustains serious injuries in Cannon Mountain crash
articles.description:
The snowboarder had been at the ski area but was out of bounds at the time of the crash.

The post Snowboarder sustains serious injuries in Cannon Mountain crash appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Snowboarder suffered serious injuries at Cannon Mountain while out of bounds.

Local authorities emphasize the importance of staying within designated areas.

articles.title: Boston police investigate stabbing near Northeastern University
articles.description:
One person was rushed to a local hospital after a stabbing on St. Botolph Street Wednesday morning, police said.

The post Boston police investigate stabbing near Northeastern University appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Police are investigating a stabbing incident on St. Botolph Street near Northeastern University.
One victim was transported to a local hospital following the Wednesday morning attack.
The incident raises safety concerns for students and residents in the Northeastern University area.
